Search Console has identified that some pages on your site are not being indexed due to the following new reason:

Alternate page with proper canonical tag

If this reason is not intentional, we recommend that you fix it in order to get affected pages indexed and appearing on Google.

**Addressing Canonical Issues**

I'm currently focused on resolving the "Alternate page with proper canonical tag" issue flagged by Search Console. I'm digging into how different URL variations, like those with and without trailing slashes, are impacting the canonical tags. My goal is to determine the best approach to standardize these pages and ensure search engines understand the preferred version. I'm exploring different implementation strategies for canonical tags, and I am close to determining the root cause of the canonicalization problem.


**Analyzing Canonical Consistency**

I've been analyzing the inconsistencies between the canonical URLs generated by my code and those present in the sitemap. It appears the `components/SEO.tsx` file normalizes URLs by removing trailing slashes and converting to lowercase, but the sitemap includes trailing slashes. This disparity could be the root cause of the "alternate" pages not being indexed, even though Google seems to have selected the "right" canonical version. I'm focusing now on reconciling these two sources.


**Investigating Root Cause**

I've discovered the homepage URL canonicalization logic in `SEO.tsx` could be the culprit. The code currently returns `https://theforgeproperties.com/` for the root path, but the sitemap lists `https://theforgeproperties.com` without a trailing slash. This difference in canonical URLs between the sitemap and the generated page content seems to be the cause of the issue flagged by Search Console, which is the "Alternate page with proper canonical tag". I need to reconcile these to establish a single source of truth, and ensure the right page is indexed.


**Aligning Sitemap & Canonicity**

payload.text:
I want you to build a secure Realtor/Agent Portal feature for The Forge Properties website.

This portal should allow real estate agents to:

Sign up and log in securely

Receive a unique referral code and referral link

Track their referrals, sales, and commissions

View analytics in a modern dashboard

Request commission payouts

The design must be premium, modern, and fintech-inspired (similar to Stripe or Paystack dashboards). Clean UI, smooth cards, subtle shadows, and professional typography.

1. Authentication System

Agent registration (Full Name, Email, Phone, Password)

Secure login/logout

Email verification

Password reset

Admin approval option before activation (optional toggle)

2. Unique Referral System

Automatically generate a unique referral code upon signup

Create auto-generated referral link:
forgeproperties.ng/ref/{agent-code}

Add copy-to-clipboard button

Track:

Total clicks

Leads generated

Converted sales

3. Dashboard Layout (After Login)
Top Overview Cards:

Total Sales Closed

Total Commission Earned

Pending Commission

Active Referrals

Analytics Section:

Line chart: Commission over time

Bar chart: Monthly sales

Pie chart: Property types sold

Sales Table:

Columns:

Client Name

Property Name

Deal Status (Pending / Under Review / Approved / Paid)

Commission Amount

Payment Status

4. Commission Wallet System

Show:

Available balance

Pending balance

Total earned

Withdrawal request button

Withdrawal history table

Minimum withdrawal limit (admin configurable)

5. Admin Panel Features

Admin must be able to:

View all agents

Approve/reject agents

Add sales manually

Assign commission to agent

Update deal status

Approve payouts

View performance leaderboard

6. Design Requirements

Modern fintech dashboard UI

Dark mode with gold or deep blue accent

Responsive (mobile + desktop)

Clean cards with soft shadows

Smooth animations

7. Security Requirements

Role-based access (Admin / Agent)

Protect routes

Secure referral tracking

Prevent commission manipulation

Database validation

8. Tech Recommendations

Use a scalable architecture:

Frontend: React / Next.js

Backend: Node.js / Express or Firebase

Database: PostgreSQL or MongoDB

Authentication: JWT or Firebase Auth

Charts: Chart.js or Recharts

Ensure the system is scalable for 1,000+ agents.

Deliver:

Database schema

API structure

Folder structure

Full working dashboard layout

Referral tracking logic

Build it production-ready.

9. Agent WhatsApp Group Integration

After successful registration, redirect agents to a “Registration Successful” page.

Display a prominent button:
“Join Official Forge Agents WhatsApp Group”

Button should open the official WhatsApp group link in a new tab.

The WhatsApp link must only be visible to logged-in agents.

Also display the WhatsApp group join button inside the dashboard as a dismissible banner.

Admin should be able to update the WhatsApp group link from the admin panel.

**Developing Core Agent Features**

I'm now zeroing in on the essential features for the "Realtor/Agent Portal." Specifically, I'm fleshing out the agent registration and login authentication (Auth) system. After that, I'm designing a unique referral system that will include personalized code and link generation, and click/lead tracking. Finally, I'll build the agent dashboard, with overviews, analytics and a sales table.


**Expanding Agent Capabilities**

I've been concentrating on the agent's financial tools, specifically the commission wallet, enabling balance tracking, and withdrawal requests. Simultaneously, I'm integrating an admin panel to manage agents, commissions, and payouts. A WhatsApp group integration for onboarding is also on the cards. Design-wise, I'm aiming for a premium, fintech aesthetic. I'm also addressing security with role-based access control.
I'm also working on database schema for agent roles, referrals, sales and payouts.
I'm updating the AuthContext.tsx file to handle agent roles. I'm also planning to refactor the PropertyContext.tsx file to handle Agents, Sales, and Payouts.


**Defining New Agent Views**

I'm creating new pages for AgentLogin, AgentSignup, AgentDashboard, AdminAgents, and AdminPayouts. I'll add an "Agent Portal" link to the Navbar. I'm also preparing to integrate Supabase for agent-related data. I'm leaning toward lightweight SVG-based charts, or simple bars for the dashboard, prioritizing speed and minimal dependencies; I may test `recharts`. I'm designing the referral link structure as `forgeproperties.ng/ref/{code}` with tracking. Finally, I'll need to define Agent, Sale, and Payout types.

### Specification for The Forge Realtor/Agent Portal

This update introduces a comprehensive, fintech-inspired Agent ecosystem. The design uses the established `forge-navy` and `forge-gold` palette with a "Glassmorphism" approach—subtle shadows, clean borders, and high-contrast typography.

#### 1. System Architecture
- **Role-Based Access**: The `AuthContext` is expanded to support two distinct login flows: Corporate Admin and Accredited Realtor.
- **Referral Engine**: A dynamic routing system handles `/ref/:code` paths, tracking engagement and redirecting to the main listings page.
- **Financial Ledger**: A virtual wallet system tracks "Pending," "Available," and "Withdrawn" commissions.

#### 2. Visual & Functional Behavior
- **Agent Dashboard**: A high-fidelity interface featuring "Stripe-style" stats cards, SVG-based responsive analytics charts, and a detailed sales ledger.
- **Referral Hub**: A dedicated section for generating and copying high-converting referral links with real-time click/lead metrics.
- **Commission Wallet**: A sleek payout interface where agents can request funds, gated by a configurable minimum balance.
- **WhatsApp Integration**: High-visibility "Join Community" banners and post-registration CTAs strictly for authenticated agents.

#### 3. New Pages & Components
- **`AgentPortal.tsx`**: Unified premium auth screen (Login/Signup).
- **`AgentDashboard.tsx`**: The main command center for Realtors.
- **`AdminAgents.tsx` & `AdminPayouts.tsx`**: New management views within the existing Admin Layout.

---

Percentage-based

Fixed amount

Deals must have the following statuses:

Pending

Approved

Paid

Logic:

When deal is created → Status = Pending

When admin verifies payment → Status = Approved
→ Commission moves to Pending Wallet

total_earned

pending_balance

available_balance

total_paid

pending_balance += commission
total_earned += commission
